Title: The morphogenesis-related NDR kinase pathway of Colletotrichum orbiculare is required for translating plant surface signals into infection-related morphogenesis and pathogenesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MOR2-PAG1 scaffolding protein; contains MOR2-PAG1_N, MOR2-PAG1_mid and MOR2-PAG1_C domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: host plant cuticle exudate on cucumber cotyledon surface
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as a plant-derived signal molecule that induces appressorium development and infection-related morphogenesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for fungal response to cutin monomer signals; loss of CoPAG1 blocks normal appressorium morphogenesis in the plant-derived chemical signaling pathway and prevents n-octadecanal-dependent activation of CoCbk1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutant screening; targeted gene deletion; complementation; constitutively active CoCbk1 rescue; phospho-western blot of CoCbk1-GFP; yeast two-hybrid; microarray transcriptomics; pathogenicity assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function inferred during appressorium development on plant surface; CoPAG1-dependent genes induced in planta
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant surface signals; cucumber exudate; n-octadecanal; 1,16-hexadecanediol
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoPAG1 was identified from pathogenesis-deficient mutants as a key component of the plant-derived signaling pathway for appressorium morphogenesis. 2. CoPAG1 is required for CoCbk1 phosphorylation/activation and for response to cutin monomers including n-octadecanal. 3. CoPAG1 contributes to expression of plant-signal-induced genes linked to pathogenicity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for blocking perception/transduction of host cuticle-derived signals and thus preventing infection structure formation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: copag1 single mutant still forms normal appressoria on artificial surfaces and on host surface when CoKel2-dependent pathway is present; virulence only slightly reduced in single mutant
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional ortholog of Saccharomyces cerevisiae TAO3/PAG1; classified here as Common_Gene because it is a scaffold/signaling component rather than enzyme or TF

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NDR/LATS family serine-threonine protein kinase; fungal nuclear Dbf2-related kinase-like catalytic domain; hydrophobic motif
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: host plant cuticle exudate on cucumber cotyledon surface
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as a host-derived signal that promotes appressorium development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CoCbk1 activity is essential for infection-structure morphogenesis and pathogenesis; its phosphorylation/activity is enhanced by n-octadecanal in a CoPag1-dependent manner
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: analog-sensitive mutant with 1NA-PP1 inhibition; constitutively active phosphomimetic mutant; phospho-western blot; pathogenicity assay; transcriptome profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Acts during conidial germination and appressorium development
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 1NA-PP1 inhibitor; plant surface; n-octadecanal; cucumber exudate
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Conditional inhibition of CoCbk1 caused bilateral germination, abnormal appressoria, and loss of pathogenicity. 2. Constitutively active CoCbk1 rescued copag1 and cohym1 morphogenesis defects, showing it acts downstream of MOR scaffolding components. 3. n-Octadecanal increases CoCbk1 phosphorylation in wild type but not in copag1 mutants.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate antifungal intervention point because kinase activity is required for infection-related morphogenesis and virulence
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive activation in wild type reduced virulence, indicating that both insufficient and excessive activity are detrimental
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a metabolic enzyme for nutrient biosynthesis; included because it is a core signal transducer controlling response to the metabolite signal n-octadecanal

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mo25-like scaffold protein with Mo25 domain; MOR/RAM component
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: host plant cuticle exudate on cucumber cotyledon surface
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as a signal molecule for appressorium development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates CoCbk1 phosphorylation/activity and is required for normal polarized germination and appressorium formation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted deletion; constitutively active CoCbk1 rescue; phospho-western blot; yeast two-hybrid
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cohym1 mutants showed defects in germination-site determination and appressorium morphogenesis similar to reduced CoCbk1 activity. 2. CoCbk1-CA restored normal appressorium formation in cohym1 mutants. 3. CoHym1 is required for full CoCbk1 phosphorylation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target within MOR signaling controlling host signal responsiveness
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion retained partial CoCbk1 phosphorylation and was less severe than full CoCbk1 loss of function
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common_Gene because it is a signaling scaffold, not enzyme or TF
